researched_ingredients: 1/2 cup dry chickpeas (or 1 can, 15 oz, drained)
1/2 large bunch curly/dinosaur kale (about 4-5 cups chopped)

items.name: Dry chickpeas
items.quantity: 1/2 cup (or 1 lb bag for multiple uses)

shopping_tips: Buy dry chickpeas instead of canned for long-term savings - a 1 lb bag costs ~$2.50 and makes 4-5 servings
Purchase tahini in larger quantities - the 16 oz jar provides 8-10 servings, reducing per-meal cost to $1-1.50
Shop store brands like Walmart Great Value, Kroger Simple Truth, or Target Good & Gather for 20-30% savings
Buy quinoa in bulk (4.5 lb Costco bag ~$12-14) to bring per-serving cost down to ~$0.50
Choose conventional kale and wash thoroughly - just as nutritious as organic at half the price
Watch for olive oil sales and stock up when 16-17 oz bottles are $4-5
Buy lemons in bags of 3-4 for better value (~$0.75 each vs $0.85-1.00 individually)
Total estimated cost: $6.50-$8.50 for 2 servings, leaving $11.50-$13.50 under your $20 budget
This meal provides 25g protein per serving, meeting high protein vegetarian requirements
Pantry staples (tahini, olive oil, spices) will lower costs for future meals since they last multiple servings
